meta.title: ClaudeChrome | Debug the Web With Browser-Aware Agents
meta.description: ClaudeChrome helps teams debug web apps, validate flows, reproduce real browser issues, and investigate user-facing bugs with Claude, Codex, and shell panes directly inside Chrome.

brand.name: ClaudeChrome
brand.tagline: Debug the web with browser-aware agents

brand.name: ClaudeChrome
brand.tagline: Local browser-aware agents for real debugging, QA, support reproduction, and release validation.

hero.title: Your browser, your agents, your actual bugs.
hero.body: ClaudeChrome puts Claude, Codex, and shell panes directly inside Chrome so teams can debug broken flows, validate releases, reproduce support tickets, and inspect real browser behavior without juggling separate terminals, tabs, and tools.

copyBlocks.title: Use it when the bug only makes sense in the browser.
copyBlocks.body: When a login loop, checkout regression, or front-end runtime error only becomes clear with the page in front of you, ClaudeChrome keeps the agent inside that context instead of forcing a manual relay between browser and terminal.

copyBlocks.title: Useful for engineering, QA, support, and release validation.
copyBlocks.body: Open a workspace, bind a pane to the exact tab you care about, inspect what the page is doing, and keep moving without losing the context that usually disappears the moment you switch tools.

commandLabel.title: Fast local setup
commandLabel.subtitle: Install once, works on any Chrome tab.

tabs.label: build
tabs.clipboard: npm run build && npm run build:host
tabs.display: npm run build && npm run build:host

metrics.title: Faster bug triage
metrics.subtitle: Keep the page, the agent, and the terminal in one place
metrics.body: Investigate request failures, console issues, and broken UI state without switching context.

metrics.title: Flow validation
metrics.subtitle: Walk real onboarding, auth, billing, and dashboard journeys
metrics.body: Useful before release, during QA signoff, and while verifying staging behavior.

metrics.title: Support reproduction
metrics.subtitle: Work against the same page state your users are dealing with
metrics.body: Bind a pane to the relevant tab, inspect what happened, and preserve the investigative thread.

metrics.title: Organized investigations
metrics.subtitle: Use workspaces and multiple panes for parallel checks
metrics.body: Compare environments, split responsibilities, or keep separate investigations from collapsing together.

eyebrow.secondary: Claude and Codex panes bound to your actual browser tab

stage.title: A live browser tab, an active pane, a real problem being solved.
stage.body: ClaudeChrome lives in the Chrome side panel. Open a workspace, bind a pane to the tab you care about, and start investigating — Claude and Codex see exactly what the browser sees.
stage.browserFrameAriaLabel: Illustration: ClaudeChrome side panel investigating a checkout flow
stage.pageRibbon: What the team is checking right now

pageCards.label: Checkout issue
pageCards.title: Find the request that failed
pageCards.body: See the exact response path that turned a working purchase flow into a support ticket.

pageCards.label: UI regression
pageCards.title: Confirm what the page actually rendered
pageCards.body: Read visible text and browser state instead of trusting screenshots or vague reports.

pageCards.label: Support
pageCards.title: Reproduce the customer path
pageCards.body: Stay on the same live tab while the pane investigates console, page content, and runtime behavior.

pageCards.label: QA signoff
pageCards.title: Validate the fix before shipping
pageCards.body: Re-run the flow, confirm the changed state, and keep the whole check inside one place.

panes.output: POST /api/checkout returned 402. Retry path and pricing payload now visible.
Billing step text captured. Error banner matches user report exactly.

notes.label: Debugging
notes.title: Triaging browser-only bugs gets easier.
notes.body: When a front-end bug depends on the current tab, its requests, and its rendered state, ClaudeChrome keeps those clues attached to the agent session instead of scattering them across windows.

notes.label: Validation
notes.title: Flow checks become more repeatable.
notes.body: Use a pane to walk an onboarding, auth, or payment flow while continuously checking what the browser is actually doing.

notes.label: Support
notes.title: Customer reports stop being vague narratives.
notes.body: Bind a pane to the relevant page, inspect the behavior, and turn “it broke for me” into a reproducible, evidence-backed investigation.

eyebrow.secondary: Real problems, solved in the browser

useCases.title: Where ClaudeChrome earns its keep.
useCases.body: Six situations where having Claude and Codex bound to your browser tab changes how the work actually gets done.

cards.label: Front-end debugging
cards.title: Investigate regressions that only reveal themselves in a live tab.
cards.body: Useful for runtime errors, broken state transitions, visual regressions, and API mismatches that are obvious in the browser but awkward to explain in a terminal-only workflow.

cards.label: Auth and onboarding
cards.title: Walk login, signup, invite, and permission flows with the browser in view.
cards.body: This matters when the success or failure of a flow depends on redirects, cookies, page text, or client-side state that a normal terminal session cannot see directly.

cards.label: Checkout and billing
cards.title: Validate high-stakes revenue paths before and after changes ship.
cards.body: Use ClaudeChrome to inspect requests, confirm rendered totals, and reproduce a broken payment step without losing the browser context that explains the failure.

cards.label: Support reproduction
cards.title: Turn vague customer tickets into concrete investigations.
cards.body: Keep a pane bound to the page that matches the customer path, inspect the browser state, and document exactly what the user saw when the issue occurred.

cards.label: Staging vs. production
cards.title: Compare environments side by side without collapsing your mental context.
cards.body: Multiple panes and workspaces make it easier to keep separate tabs, separate flows, and separate investigative threads organized while you compare behavior.

cards.label: Release confidence
cards.title: Run final browser checks before saying “ship it.”
cards.body: ClaudeChrome is valuable right before release, when one more pass through the real browser can prevent a small front-end issue from becoming a public problem.

eyebrow.secondary: Reserve visual slots for product proof, not generic decoration

visualReserve.title: Make future screenshots tell real product stories.
visualReserve.body: These slots are reserved so the page can absorb authentic product media later: real UI captures, operator walkthroughs, and `.cast` recordings anchored in practical workflows.

shots.title: Full browser window with the side panel in action
shots.body: Show the product in the exact setting where it matters: a real app page, a real pane, and a visible investigation context.

shots.title: Pane workflow around a real issue
shots.body: Capture a high-value scenario such as checkout debugging, SSO reproduction, or onboarding validation.

shots.title: Visual story of a problem, investigation, and fix
shots.body: Use this slot for a compact, buyer-friendly proof asset instead of a system architecture diagram.

eyebrow.secondary: How teams use it every day

workflows.title: Practical loops teams can run with ClaudeChrome.
workflows.body: From daily debugging to pre-release sign-off — these are the loops ClaudeChrome slots into naturally.

cards.label: Regression triage
cards.title: Open the failing page, bind a pane, inspect what broke.
cards.body: Keep requests, page content, and agent reasoning tied to the same browser context while you narrow down the issue.

cards.label: Flow walkthroughs
cards.title: Validate login, onboarding, and billing paths before release.
cards.body: Use one pane per task or environment so signoff work stays organized instead of turning into tab chaos.

cards.label: Support investigations
cards.title: Reproduce what customers describe while keeping an evidence trail.
cards.body: The product is especially valuable when the problem is visible in the browser but hard to communicate through logs alone.

cards.label: Environment comparison
cards.title: Keep staging, production, and local checks side by side.
cards.body: Multi-pane workspaces let you compare states without losing which browser tab each session is talking about.

cards.label: Human-supervised action
cards.title: Guide an agent through browser work without giving up operator control.
cards.body: Inspect first, act when needed, and verify again while the human stays in charge of the broader investigation.

cards.label: Release readiness
cards.title: Make browser validation feel like part of shipping, not a separate ritual.
cards.body: ClaudeChrome helps teams treat browser validation as part of the same operational surface where debugging and fixes already happen.

demos.title: Watch it work on real problems.
demos.body: These recordings show ClaudeChrome in the exact situations where it saves the most time — no scripted demos, no sanitised inputs.

cards.label: Checkout regression
cards.title: Find the exact request that turned a working purchase flow into a support ticket.
cards.body: Bind a pane to the checkout tab, inspect live network requests, and identify the failure point without leaving the browser.

cards.label: Support reproduction
cards.title: Turn a vague bug report into a reproducible, evidence-backed investigation.
cards.body: Bind a pane to the customer's exact page path, inspect what the browser actually did, and close the ticket with a clear answer instead of another follow-up question.

cards.label: Release validation
cards.title: Walk a critical flow and confirm it works before you ship.
cards.body: One place to navigate the browser, inspect the evidence, and get a definitive answer — without switching between DevTools, terminal, and a separate test runner.

eyebrow.secondary: Built for the people closest to the browser

teams.title: ClaudeChrome fits the teams that live closest to real browser behavior.
teams.body: If your work touches live browser behavior — debugging, QA, support, or release validation — ClaudeChrome was built for your workflow.

cards.label: Front-end engineers
cards.title: Debug what the browser is actually doing.
cards.body: Especially useful when the UI, requests, and runtime state matter as much as the source code.

cards.label: QA teams
cards.title: Keep validation and evidence in one operator surface.
cards.body: Walk test scenarios, capture the result, and keep a reliable record of how the browser behaved.

cards.label: Support engineers
cards.title: Reproduce difficult customer paths more quickly.
cards.body: Turn browser issues into inspectable investigations instead of long back-and-forth tickets.

cards.label: Product engineers
cards.title: Check launch-critical flows before and after release.
cards.body: Good for onboarding, pricing, billing, settings, and the parts of the product that break publicly first.

cards.label: Consultants and agencies
cards.title: Show clients a visible, high-signal investigation process.
cards.body: The product helps turn diagnosis and validation into something presentable, not just something done offscreen.

cards.label: Founders and solo builders
cards.title: Move between building, checking, and fixing without tool sprawl.
cards.body: When one person does everything, ClaudeChrome reduces the friction between browser checks and implementation work.

faq.title: What people will ask before they try it.

items.question: Is ClaudeChrome mainly a debugging tool?
items.answer: Debugging is the clearest entry point, but the product is equally useful for QA walkthroughs, support reproduction, release validation, and any workflow where the browser itself carries the important evidence.

items.question: Why not just use DevTools and a normal terminal side by side?
items.answer: Because the value is not just having both tools open. It is keeping the agent session attached to the relevant browser tab so the investigation thread stays coherent while you move between inspection and action.

items.question: Do I need a full automation setup to benefit from it?
items.answer: No. ClaudeChrome is useful earlier than that. It helps the moment you want a browser-aware agent workspace for real pages, real flows, and real validation work.

items.question: Does it work on any website, or just local dev environments?
items.answer: Any tab Chrome has open. ClaudeChrome binds to the active tab, so it works equally well on localhost, staging, production, or a third-party site you are investigating for a client.

eyebrow.secondary: Open source, runs locally, up in minutes

finalCta.title: The fastest way to understand ClaudeChrome is to use it on a real problem.
finalCta.body: Install takes minutes. Once you have a pane bound to a live tab, the value is immediate — no configuration ceremony, no synthetic test environment required.

footer.tagline: ClaudeChrome · A browser-aware workspace for debugging, QA, support reproduction, and release validation inside Chrome.

meta.description: ClaudeChrome 将 Claude、Codex 和 Shell 面板直接嵌入 Chrome，帮助团队调试 Web 应用、验证流程、复现真实浏览器问题。

brand.name: ClaudeChrome
brand.tagline: 用浏览器感知的 AI 智能体调试 Web 应用

brand.name: ClaudeChrome
brand.tagline: 本地化浏览器感知 AI 智能体，专为调试、QA、复现支持工单和发布验证而生。

eyebrow.secondary: 浏览器感知的 AI 智能体，直接嵌入 Chrome

hero.title: 你的浏览器，你的智能体，你的真实 Bug。
hero.body: ClaudeChrome 将 Claude、Codex 和 Shell 面板直接嵌入 Chrome，让团队无需在终端、标签页和工具之间来回切换，便可调试故障流程、验证发布、复现支持工单，并检查真实的浏览器行为。

copyBlocks.title: 当 Bug 只有在浏览器里才说得清楚时，它最有用。
copyBlocks.body: 当登录死循环、结账回归或前端运行时错误只有在页面打开时才能复现，ClaudeChrome 让智能体留在那个上下文中，而不是在浏览器和终端之间来回传递信息。

copyBlocks.title: 适用于工程、QA、支持和发布验证。
copyBlocks.body: 打开工作区，将面板绑定到你关心的标签页，检查页面行为，持续推进——不再因切换工具而丢失上下文。

useCases.title: ClaudeChrome 在这些场景中体现其价值。

cards.label: 前端调试
cards.title: 排查只有在实时标签页中才会暴露的回归问题。
cards.body: 适用于运行时错误、状态跳转异常、视觉回归和 API 不匹配——这些问题在浏览器中一目了然，却很难在纯终端工作流中说清楚。

cards.title: 在浏览器可见的情况下，走过登录、注册、邀请和权限流程。
cards.body: 当流程的成败取决于重定向、Cookie、页面文本或普通终端会话无法直接看到的客户端状态时，这一点尤为重要。

cards.label: 结账与账单
cards.title: 在变更上线前后验证高风险的收入路径。
cards.body: 使用 ClaudeChrome 检查请求、确认渲染金额，并在不丢失解释故障的浏览器上下文的情况下复现支付环节的问题。

cards.label: 支持工单复现
cards.title: 将模糊的客户工单转化为具体的调查。
cards.body: 将面板绑定到与用户路径匹配的页面，检查浏览器状态，准确记录用户在问题发生时看到了什么。

cards.label: 预发布 vs. 生产
cards.title: 并排比较不同环境，同时保持清晰的心理模型。
cards.body: 多个面板和工作区让你在比较行为时，能够有条不紊地管理独立的标签页、流程和调查线索。

cards.label: 发布信心
cards.title: 在说「可以上线」之前，完成最后的浏览器检查。
cards.body: ClaudeChrome 在发布前尤为有价值——在真实浏览器中多走一遍，可以防止小的前端问题演变成公开的生产事故。

cards.label: 创始人与独立开发者
cards.title: 在构建、检查和修复之间切换，无需工具蔓延。
cards.body: 当一个人承担所有工作时，ClaudeChrome 降低了浏览器检查与实现工作之间的摩擦。

eyebrow.secondary: 先回答大家最关心的问题

faq.title: 用户在尝试之前最常问的问题。

items.question: ClaudeChrome 主要是一个调试工具吗？
items.answer: 调试是最清晰的切入点，但该产品同样适用于 QA 走查、支持工单复现、发布验证，以及任何浏览器本身承载重要证据的工作流。

items.question: 为什么不直接并排使用 DevTools 和普通终端？
items.answer: 因为价值不仅仅在于同时打开两个工具。关键在于将智能体会话附加到相关的浏览器标签页，使调查线索在检查与操作之间保持连贯。

items.question: 我需要完整的自动化环境才能使用它吗？
items.answer: 不需要。ClaudeChrome 在更早的阶段就能发挥作用。当你需要一个浏览器感知的智能体工作区来处理真实页面、真实流程和真实验证工作时，它就能帮上忙。

items.question: 它适用于任何网站，还是仅限于本地开发环境？
items.answer: 适用于 Chrome 中打开的任何标签页。ClaudeChrome 绑定到活动标签页，因此在 localhost、预发布环境、生产环境或你为客户调查的第三方网站上同样有效。

eyebrow.secondary: 开源、本地运行、几分钟即可上手

finalCta.title: 了解 ClaudeChrome 最快的方式，就是用它解决一个真实问题。
finalCta.body: 安装只需几分钟。一旦将面板绑定到实时标签页，价值立竿见影——无需繁琐配置，也不需要搭建合成测试环境。

footer.tagline: ClaudeChrome · 在 Chrome 中完成真实的调试、QA、支持工单复现和发布验证。
